resources.string: Hooks Private Compute Services to provide an alternative way to download models
Public Compute Services does not come with a default repository, you must enter one yourself. Not sure what this is or where to find one? Check the GitHub.
Enter the URL to load manifests from (required)
Enter the URL to load manifests from. Not sure what this is? Check the GitHub.
Could not load manifest, check your connection and that you entered the right URL

Your device does not have a default device configuration set. Please select the one for your device below.\nConfigurations that are known to be for a device are listed, but may are not known. If your device is using an unknown configuration by default, please reply to the XDA thread with it to help build a better list!
Changing your device configuration may cause AI models to stop updating, or stop working entirely. Please select the correct configuration for your device below.
Set the device configuration to %1s, %1s? Please note that setting the wrong device\'s configuration may result in AI models not being downloadable, or downloaded models not working.
You may need to clear the data of apps which are using the downloaded AI models, as well as AIcore, for new models to download.
This will reset the device configuration to the default one for your device - this may be no configuration, in which case you will be asked to pick a configuration again.
You have the latest manifests available from the repository
Manifest updates are available from the repository, tap to sync
An error occurred downloading manifest updates, tap to try again
An update for Public Compute Services is available (%1s to %1s), tap to view
Change the device configuration to use for AI models
Change the repository to download manifests from
Automatically check for updates once a day, and sync manifest updates automatically. Apps using these models will then download model updates automatically in the background, which may use data.
Tweaks & experiments for PCS-powered AI apps
Enable debug logging for hooked apps.\nNote: You must also enable the Xposed module for apps you wish to have extra logging for, such as AICore or Device Intelligence.
Force stop hooked apps for this change to take effect
Questions and answers about Public Compute Services
These options may cause crashes or errors, use at your own risk
Enable features in Google Phone, even if they are not supported on this device or in this region. May cause crashes or errors, use with caution.\nNote: Voice Translate is not currently supported.
Show Google Tasks & Google Wallet in the app data list\nNote: These sources may not work yet. Please reply to the XDA thread if you get them working!
Allow access to the Magic Cue settings without a Google account. Other features likely won\'t work, but this is useful for testing.
Allow Magic Cue to work on all Google account types
Allow Magic Cue to work if your device administrator has disallowed content suggestions and content capture\nNote: I take no responsibility if this gets you fired, use at your own risk.
Contains code from Private Compute Services, under the Apache-2.0 licence
Licenced under the SIL Open Font Licence, Version 1.1
Notifications shown when there is an update for Public Compute Services
An update is available for Public Compute Services (%1s to %1s)
Root is not granted, please grant root to Public Compute Services.
The Xposed module is not enabled. Please make sure you have enabled the Xposed module and rebooted.
